name: Judd Trail (Jackass Ginger Pool)
tagline: A quick shady loop through Nu'uanu Valley with a hidden swimming hole the locals call Jackass Ginger.
parking: Free roadside parking along Nu'uanu Pali Drive near the trailhead — limited spots
bestTime: Midday when you want a quick shady escape from the heat
talkStory: Named after Charles S. Judd, a territorial forester who planted these eucalyptus and Norfolk pines in the 1930s to reforest Nu'uanu after decades of logging. The 'Jackass Ginger' pool got its name from the wild yellow ginger growing along the stream and the donkeys that once roamed these hills. For generations local kids have used the muddy hillside above the pool as a natural mud slide — it's a rite of passage. Swim at your own risk though: leptospirosis is a real concern in all Oahu freshwater.
